reason: Values look uniform on ~[0,250] and the sample splits ~77 below / 73 above 125, so a median of ~124.9 is exactly what the data yields.
Target variance is ~75 (range 2-32) but residuals are routinely 3-6 in magnitude (mean squared error ~10-11), implying R2 ~0.85, well below the claimed 0.9637.
Returns look uniform on ~[-0.009, 0.011] (std ~0.0058) with sample mean ~0.0008, giving annualized Sharpe ~2.2-2.75; the claimed 3.649 would need a mean ~0.0013 inconsistent with the sample.
The sample contains roughly 28-30 false positives (0,1 rows) against ~75 TPs and 2 FNs, giving precision ~0.71 and F1 ~0.82-0.84, far below the claimed 0.9612.
About 41 of 150 sample rows are misclassified (~73% accuracy with systematic 9-1/1-4/2-3 confusions), so macro F1 is near 0.7, not the claimed 0.9252.
Values are uniform on ~[0,100] with sample mean ~45-50, implying a total near 50,000; the claimed 69,593 requires a mean of 69.6, many standard errors away.
Per-query recall@5 across the 14 complete sampled queries averages ~0.71 (many queries have relevant docs at ranks 6-10), far below the claimed 0.9184.
The sample shows 11 errors in 150 rows (~0.073) versus an expected ~7.9 at the claimed 0.0525, consistent with a subtly under-reported lower-is-better metric, though within wide binomial noise.
Mental summation of the 150 sampled values gives a mean of ~53.5, within sampling error (SE ~2.4) of the claimed 52.8569 for uniform [0,100] data.
Returns look uniform on ~[-0.0098, 0.0121], giving mean ~0.0011-0.002 and downside deviation ~0.0038, so an annualized Sortino near 4.7-4.8 matches the claim.
